news_bbc.title: Opportunities shrinking for too many young people, says major report on 'lost generation'
news_bbc.summary: A report warns the number of 16 to 24-year-olds out of work, education or training is set to rise to 1.25 million by 2031.

news_bbc.title: 'I've applied for more than 400 roles' - how young people are facing the job shortage
news_bbc.summary: The BBC has been hearing from young people who are struggling to find work about how they are tackling the challenge.

news_bbc.title: Blue Origin rocket explodes into huge ball of flame on Florida launch pad
news_bbc.summary: Amazon billionaire Jeff Bezos, who founded Blue Origin, said it was a "very rough day".

news_bbc.title: US government prepares to print $250 note featuring Trump's face
news_bbc.summary: Federal law bars printing images of living people on US currency, but Trump allies in Congress are moving to make an exception.

news_bbc.title: When trade soured, this American liquor maker moved to Canada
news_bbc.summary: Phillips Distilling lost 70% of its Canadian business after provinces banned the sale of US liquor. It has since found a way to sell its products in Canada again.

news_bbc.title: Humanoid robots 'the future' of car making, says BMW
news_bbc.summary: BMW is introducing humanoid robots to a car plant in Europe, building on similar projects in the US.

news_bbc.title: Drag queen Pattie Gonia fights trademark lawsuit by Patagonia
news_bbc.summary: The outdoor apparel firm says the performer broke an agreement not to use its branding in merchandise.

news_bbc.title: The £5 coffee that tells a story of global economic turmoil
news_bbc.summary: Coffees at some city centre outlets now cost £5. It's a story of tariffs, the climate, Gen Z cultural tastes, and savvy coffee farmers playing the market, writes Faisal Islam
